package.name: NEOIPC_CORE_TRK_0.1.0-alpha_DHIS2.40.3.2-en
package.description: NeoIPC Core surveillance tracker program and its configuration dependencies (data elements, generated option sets, program rules and variables, tracked-entity type and attributes, analytics groups, user groups and roles). Install base: no org-unit hierarchy and no users. ALPHA / pre-standards: not yet conformant to the WHO dhis2-package-exporter sharing and manifest conventions.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Admission on day of life
dataElements.description: For infants that have not been delivered in your own hospital, record the infant's day of life on the day of admission (day of birth = day of life 1. The next day, starting at 00:00, is the second day of life.)

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Admission Length of Stay
dataElements.description: Length of Stay (should always be 0). For Reporting purpose only

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Admission type
dataElements.description: Describes if the infant was born in your hospital or if it was admitted after birth and if so, how long after birth.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Antibiotic treatment
dataElements.formName: Intravenous antibiotic therapy for five or more days initiated
dataElements.description: Antibiotic treatment for at least five days was initiated. The day of the first dose and the day of the last dose are counted. Days where no dose was administered between the first and the last dose (e.g., skipped doses because of high drug levels in therapeutic drug monitoring) are counted as if a dose had been administered. Days after the last dose are not counted regardless of the patient's measured or assumed drug level. If the infant died, was discharged, or transferred before the end of the five-day course of intravenous antibiotics, this condition is met if treatment was scheduled for five days or more.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Acidosis
dataElements.description: Unexplained metabolic acidosis with a base deficit greater than 10 mmol/L (base excess more negative than -10 mmol/L).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Apnoea
dataElements.formName: New/more frequent episodes of apnea or increases in oxygen demand or ventilatory support
dataElements.description: New or increased frequency of episodes of apnea lasting more than 20 seconds, or an increase in the amount of oxygen required to maintain adequate oxygenation, or an escalation of ventilatory support, such as increased flow with high-flow therapy or intubation for invasive ventilation.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Bradycardias
dataElements.description: New/more frequent episodes of bradycardia with a heart rate of less than 80 beats per minute or otherwise unexplained tachycardia with a resting heart rate of more than 200 beats per minute.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI CRP
dataElements.description: There is at least one laboratory test result with a C-reactive protein (CRP) value of more than 10 mg/L (1 mg/dL).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Device association
dataElements.description: An infection is considered as vascular catheter-associated if the catheter has been present for at least three consecutive days on the day of infection (=first symptoms or first positive diagnostic test; e.g. culture) or the day before. Both, central venous catheter (CVC) association and peripheral venous catheter (PVC) association are recorded. If a patient developing a bloodstream infection meets the criteria for both, PVC- and CVC-association, the CVC is considered as the device with the relatively higher infection risk and the BSI should be recorded as CVC-associated BSI.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Day of life
dataElements.description: The day of life on which the bloodstream infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Feeding intolerance
dataElements.formName: Enteral feeding intolerance, abdominal distension or ileus
dataElements.description: Enteral feeding intolerance, abdominal distension or ileus without imaging findings or surgical findings that would otherwise suggest a necrotizing entrocolitis or a spontaneous intestinal perforation.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Hyperglycaemia
dataElements.description: New and unexplained hyperglycaemia (> 140 mg/dl or > 7.8 mmol/L) or hypoglycaemia (<40 mg/dl or < 2.2 mmol/L).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Interleukin
dataElements.description: Increased level of interleukin (IL) 6 or 8.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Irritability
dataElements.formName: Irritability, lethargy, apathy or unstable condition
dataElements.description: Irritability, lethargy, apathy or unstable condition.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI I/T-Ratio
dataElements.description: I/T-Ratio (ratio of immature granulocytes to total granulocytes) > 0.2.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Length of Stay
dataElements.description: The Length of Stay on which the bloodstream infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI No positive culture
dataElements.description: No blood culture taken or negative blood culture.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1
dataElements.description: The first organism detected in a blood culture.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 3GCR
dataElements.description: The organism is a third-generation cephalosporin resistant (3GCR) gram-negative organism (sometimes also called ESBL/AmpC). Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for third-generation cephalosporin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for third-generation cephalosporin resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 carbapenem-resistant
dataElements.description: The organism is a carbapenem-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for carbapenem-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem, and the result was negative in all cases. Select "Not tested" if no test for carbapenem-resistance (for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem) was performed. Carbapenemase-producing organisms are considered as carbapenem-resistant irrespective of the actual phenotypical resistance to individual carbapenems.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 colistin-resistant
dataElements.description: The organism is a colistin-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for colistin-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for colistin-resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 MRSA
dataElements.description: The organism is a methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA). Select "Yes" if a MRSA-test was performed on the isolate and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if noMRSA-test was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 recovered multiple times
dataElements.description: The organism was recovered from at least two blood culture and/or CSF culture specimens, collected on separate occasions.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 name
dataElements.description: The name of the organism. Please only use this option if the organism is not contained in the organism-list above. The admin team will be informed about any newly-added organism name and will reassign them once the organism has been added to the list.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 source
dataElements.description: The material the organism has been recovered from.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 VRE
dataElements.description: The organism is a vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus(VRE). Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for vancomycin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for vancomycin resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Perfusion
dataElements.description: Capillary refill time (CRF) of > 3s, skin mottling or core/peripheral temperature gap > 2 °C

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Platelet count
dataElements.description: Platelet count < 100 ×10⁹/L (100 ×10³/µL)

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Procalcitonin
dataElements.description: Procalcitonin ≥ 2μg/L (2 ng/mL; 200 ng/dL)

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI Temperature
dataElements.description: Fever (> 38 °C) or hypothermia (< 36.5 °C) or temperature instability.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC BSI White blood cell count
dataElements.description: White blood cell count > 4 ×10⁹/L (4000/µL)

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Bradycardias
dataElements.description: New/more frequent episodes of bradycardia with a heart rate of less than 80 beats per minute or otherwise unexplained tachycardia with a resting heart rate of more than 200 beats per minute.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Device association
dataElements.description: A Pneumonia is  is considered as device-associated if the patient had invasive ventilation or non-invasive ventilatory support for at least 3 consecutive days on the day of infection (=first symptoms or first positive culture) or the day before. Ventilaton via endotracheal or tracheostomy tube is considered as invasive ventilation. CPAP or High-Flow Nasal Cannula is considered as non-invasive ventilation. Note: If both invasive and non-invasive ventilation were used intermittently, a pneumonia should be recorded as INV-associated pneumonia.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Day of life
dataElements.description: The day of life on which the pneumonia occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Fever
dataElements.description: Fever (> 38 °C) or hypothermia (< 36.5 °C) or temperature instability.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Imaging findings
dataElements.description: At least one of the following imaging findings (imaging technologies: X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound) shows new changes suggestive of pneumonia, such as: infiltrate, shadowing, opacification, increased density, fluid in the intrapleural cavity or interlobar fissure.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Increased respiratory secretion
dataElements.description: More frequent endotrachel suctioning required.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Laboratory findings
dataElements.description: There is at least one laboratory test result with a C-reactive protein (CRP) value of more than 20 mg/L (2 mg/dL) or an increased level of interleukin (IL) 6 or 8

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Length of Stay
dataElements.description: The Length of Stay on which the pneumonia occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Microbiological test result
dataElements.description: Availability of test results from a culture- or non-culture based microbiologic test from the respiratory tract specimen.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Purulent tracheal aspirate
dataElements.description: Purulent tracheal aspirate

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Respiratory distress
dataElements.description: New or more frequent symptoms of respiratory distress (retractions, nasal flaring, grunting).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Respiratory support
dataElements.formName: Beginning or increase in respiratory support
dataElements.description: New initiation of respiratory support or escalation of existing level of respiratory support* for ≥ 2 days after at least two days of stability.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary bloodstream infection
dataElements.description: A BSI that is thought to be seeded from this hospital-acquired pneumonia. A BSI can be attributed to a site-specific infection (in this case the hospital-acquired pneumonia) if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first symptoms of site-specific infection), 3 days prior, and 13 days after.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary BSI organism 3 3GCR
dataElements.description: The organism is a third-generation cephalosporin resistant (3GCR) gram-negative organism (sometimes also called ESBL/AmpC). Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for third-generation cephalosporin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for third-generation cephalosporin resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary BSI organism 3 carbapenem-resistant
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC HAP Sec. BSI org. 3 carb.-res.
dataElements.description: The organism is a carbapenem-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for carbapenem-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem, and the result was negative in all cases. Select "Not tested" if no test for carbapenem-resistance (for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem) was performed. Carbapenemase-producing organisms are considered as carbapenem-resistant irrespective of the actual phenotypical resistance to individual carbapenems.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary BSI organism 3 colistin-resistant
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC HAP Sec. BSI org. 3 col.-res.
dataElements.description: The organism is a colistin-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for colistin-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for colistin-resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary BSI organism 3 MRSA
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC HAP Sec. BSI org. 3 MRSA
dataElements.description: The organism is a methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA). Select "Yes" if a MRSA-test was performed on the isolate and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if noMRSA-test was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary BSI organism 3 name
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC HAP Sec. BSI org. 3 name
dataElements.description: The name of the organism. Please only use this option if the organism is not contained in the organism-list above. The admin team will be informed about any newly-added organism name and will reassign them once the organism has been added to the list.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Secondary BSI organism 3 VRE
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC HAP Sec. BSI org. 3 VRE
dataElements.description: The organism is a vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus(VRE). Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for vancomycin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for vancomycin resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC HAP Tachypnoea
dataElements.description: New or increased frequency of episodes of apnea lasting more than 20 seconds, or new or more frequent tachypnoea (>60/min).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Abdominal distension
dataElements.description: Distended abdomen.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Abdominal discoloration
dataElements.description: Abdominal discoloration or shiny/reddish skin tone.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Bilious gastric aspirate
dataElements.description: Bilious gastric aspirate (not from transpyloric feeding tube).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Blood in stool
dataElements.formName: Occult or visible blood in stool
dataElements.description: Repeated occult (guaiac test) or visible blood in stool (no anal fissure).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Extensive bowel necrosis
dataElements.description: Surgical or pathologic finding of extensive bowel necrosis (>2 cm bowel affected).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Day of life
dataElements.description: The day of life on which the NEC occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Fixed bowel loops
dataElements.description: Fixed bowel loops (>= 24h).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Increased gastric residuals
dataElements.description: Increased gastric residuals from previous feeding.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Length of Stay
dataElements.description: The Length of Stay on which the NEC occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis imaging
dataElements.description: Pneumatosis intestinalis in imaging findings (imaging technologies: X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis surgical
dataElements.formName: Pneumatosis intestinalis with or without intestinal perforation
dataElements.description: Surgical or pathologic finding of pneumatosis intestinalis with or without intestinal perforation.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumoperitoneum
dataElements.description: Pneumoperitoneum in imaging findings (imaging technologies: X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Portal venous gas
dataElements.description: Accumulation of gas(-bubbles) in the portal vein and its branches.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Secondary bloodstream infection
dataElements.description: A BSI that is thought to be seeded from this NEC A BSI can be attributed to a site-specific infection (in this case the NEC if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first symptoms of site-specific infection), 3 days prior, and 13 days after.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC NEC Vomiting
dataElements.description: Increasing/pronounced vomiting (e.g. bilious or bloody).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Abscess deep incision
dataElements.formName: Abscess or other evidence of infection involving the deep incision
dataElements.description: An abscess or other evidence of infection involving the deep incision that is detected on gross anatomical or histopathologic exam, or imaging test.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Abscess organ space
dataElements.formName: Abscess or other evidence of infection involving the organ/space
dataElements.description: An abscess or other evidence of infection involving the organ/space that is detected on gross anatomical or histopathologic exam, or imaging test evidence suggestive of infection.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Day of life
dataElements.description: The day of life on which the surgical site infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Fever
dataElements.description: Fever (> 38 °C) or hypothermia (< 36.5 °C) or temperature instability.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Deep incision dehisces
dataElements.formName: Deep incision dehisces, opened or aspirated
dataElements.description: A deep incision that spontaneously dehisces, or is deliberately opened or aspirated by a surgeon, physician or physician designee.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Superficial incision opened
dataElements.description: Superficial incision is deliberately opened by a surgeon, physician or physician designee

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Infection present
dataElements.formName: Infection present at time of surgery
dataElements.description: Select "Yes" only if the evidence of infection seen (seen) during the surgical procedure applies to the depth of the SSI attributed to the procedure. For example, if a patient has a documented intra-abdominal infection at the time of surgery and later returns with an organ/space infection, Set "Infection present at the time of surgery" to "Yes". If a patient has a documented intra-abdominal infection at the time of surgery and later returns with superficial or deep incisional infection, select "No" for "Infection present at time of surgery".

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Infection type
dataElements.description: A superficial incisional SSI involves only skin and subcutaneous tissue of the incision. A deep incisional SSI involves deep soft tissues of the incision (for example, fascial and muscle layers). An organ/space SSI involves any part of the body deeper than the fascial/muscle layers that is opened or manipulated during the operative procedure.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Localized erythema
dataElements.description: A localized erythema in the area of the incision.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Localized heat
dataElements.description: Localized heat in the area of the incision.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Localized pain deep
dataElements.description: Localized pain or tenderness in the area of the incision.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Localized pain superficial
dataElements.description: Localized pain or tenderness in the area of the incision.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Localized swelling
dataElements.description: Localized swelling in the area of the incision.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI length of stay
dataElements.description: The length of stay  on which the surgical site infection occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from deep tissue of incision
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Organisms from deep tissue
dataElements.formName: Organism(s) identified from deep tissue of incision
dataElements.description: At least one organism has been identified from the deep tissue of the incision by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, NOT Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from organ space fluid tissue
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Organisms from organ space fluid tissue
dataElements.formName: Organism(s) identified from organ/space fluid or tissue
dataElements.description: At least one organism has been identified from fluid or tissue in the organ/space by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, NOT Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from superficial incision
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Organisms from superficial incision
dataElements.description: At least one organism has been identified from an aseptically obtained specimen from the superficial incision or subcutaneous tissue by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, NOT Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).

dataElements.description: The second pathogen detected in a blood culture in a secondary sepsis.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 2 3GCR
dataElements.description: The organism is a third-generation cephalosporin resistant (3GCR) gram-negative organism (sometimes also called ESBL/AmpC). Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for third-generation cephalosporin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for third-generation cephalosporin resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 2 carbapenem-resistant
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Sec. BSI org. 2 carb.-res.
dataElements.description: The organism is a carbapenem-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for carbapenem-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem, and the result was negative in all cases. Select "Not tested" if no test for carbapenem-resistance (for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem) was performed. Carbapenemase-producing organisms are considered as carbapenem-resistant irrespective of the actual phenotypical resistance to individual carbapenems.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 2 colistin-resistant
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Sec. BSI org. 2 col.-res.
dataElements.description: The organism is a colistin-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for colistin-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for colistin-resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 2 MRSA
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Sec. BSI org. 2 MRSA
dataElements.description: The organism is a methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA). Select "Yes" if a MRSA-test was performed on the isolate and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if noMRSA-test was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 2 name
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Sec. BSI org. 2 name
dataElements.description: The name of the organism. Please only use this option if the organism is not contained in the organism-list above. The admin team will be informed about any newly-added organism name and will reassign them once the organism has been added to the list.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 2 VRE
dataElements.shortName: NeoIPC SSI Sec. BSI org. 2 VRE
dataElements.description: The organism is a vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus(VRE). Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for vancomycin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for vancomycin resistance was performed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 3
dataElements.description: The third pathogen detected in a blood culture in a secondary sepsis.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SSI Secondary BSI organism 3 3GCR

Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for third-generation cephalosporin resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested and the result was negative. Select "Not tested" if no test for third-generation cephalosporin resistance was performed.

dataElements.description: The organism is a carbapenem-resistant gram-negative organism. Select "Yes" if the isolate was tested for carbapenem-resistance and the result was positive. Select "No" if it was tested for at least one of Imipenem, Meropenem, or Ertapenem, and the result was negative in all cases. dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery ASA-Score
dataElements.description: The American Society of Anesthesiologists (ASA) Physical Status Classification System to assess and communicate a patient’s pre-anesthesia medical co-morbidities.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SURGERY Day of life
dataElements.description: The day of life on which the surgery occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Duration
dataElements.description: The duration of the surgical procedure in minutes (incision-to-suture time if available).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Emergency procedure
dataElements.description: Yes: A procedure that is documented per the facility’s protocol to be an emergency or urgent procedure. No: The intervention is initiated and performed in a planned manner Unknown: No information available.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Endoscopic procedure
dataElements.description: Select "Yes" if the procedure was performed completely endoscopically and "No" if the procedure was performed open or endoscopically assisted, or when switching to an open technique during an endoscopic procedure occurred.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Implant
dataElements.description: An implant is a foreign body of non-human origin that is permanently placed into a patient during an operation and is not routinely manipulated for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes (e.g.; vascular prostheses, screws, wires, meshes).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Infection signs
dataElements.formName: Signs of infection at time of surgery
dataElements.description: Complete this field if signs of infection were identified during the surgical procedure. The signs of infection must be noted intraoperatively and documented in the narrative part of the operation note or report of surgery. If a surgical site infection occurs, this information will be used to determine if the signs listed in this section can be interpreted as "Infection present at time of surgery".

dataElements.name: NeoIPC SURGERY Length of Stay
dataElements.description: The Length of Stay on which the surgery occurred. This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Main procedure code
dataElements.description: The International Classification of Health Interventions (ICHI) code of the main procedure performed. If multiple different procedures are performed during one surgery, the surgeon decides which one is the main procedure (typically the most complex procedure or the on causing the highest risk for infection).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Primary closure
dataElements.description: The closure of the skin level during the original surgery, regardless of the presence of wires, wicks, drains, or other devices or objects extruding through the incision. This category includes surgeries where the skin is closed by some means. Thus, if any portion of the incision is closed at the skin level, by any manner, a designation of primary closure should be assigned to the surgery. Note: If a procedure has multiple incision/laparoscopic trocar sites and any of the incisions are closed primarily then the procedure technique is recorded as primary closed.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Procedure description
dataElements.description: A human-readable name or description of the surgical procedure as it it typically called by surgeons in your institution (e.g.; ligation of patent arterial duct).

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Revision procedure
dataElements.description: Revision procedures are follow-up, replacement or corrective procedures after an initial procedure. A revision procedure terminates the follow-up for the primary procedure and starts a new follwo-up period.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Side procedure code 1
dataElements.description: The International Classification of Health Interventions (ICHI) code of a side procedure performed. If multiple different procedures are performed during one surgery, the surgeon decides which one is the main procedure (typically the most complex procedure or the on causing the highest risk for infection) and which ones are side procedures.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Side procedure code 2
dataElements.description: The International Classification of Health Interventions (ICHI) code of a side procedure performed. If multiple different procedures are performed during one surgery, the surgeon decides which one is the main procedure (typically the most complex procedure or the on causing the highest risk for infection) and which ones are side procedures.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surgery Wound class
dataElements.description: An assessment of the degree of contamination of a surgical wound at the time of the surgical procedure. Wound class is assigned by a person involved in the surgical procedure (for example, surgeon, circulating nurse, etc.). The four wound classifications are: Clean, Clean-Contaminated, Contaminated, and Infected.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days when the infant received (any) systemic antibiotics. Only one antibiotic day can be recorded per day, which mans that a day when the patient received multiple antibiotics is still counted as one antibiotic day.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01
dataElements.description: The first systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 1.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02
dataElements.description: The second systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 2.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03
dataElements.description: The third systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 3.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04
dataElements.description: The fourth systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 4.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05
dataElements.description: The fifth systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 5.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06
dataElements.description: The sixth systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 6.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07
dataElements.description: The seventh systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 7.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08
dataElements.description: The eighth systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 8.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 09
dataElements.description: The ninth systemic antibiotic substance the infant received.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 09 days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the infant received the systemic antibiotic substance specified as antbiotic substance 9.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end CVC days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days when a central venous catheter was in place for at least 12 hours/day.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Human milk days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the patient’s enteral feeding exclusively consists of (own mother’s or donor) breast milk. Fortified breast milk is considered as breast milk.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end INV days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days when the infant was on invasive ventilation (intubated) for at least 12 hours/day.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Kangaroo care days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the patient received kangaroo care (intensive skin-to-skin-contact) for at least 2 hours.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end NIV days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days when the infant was on non-invasive ventilation (not intubated; e.g. high flow nasal cannulae) for at least 12 hours/day.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days
dataElements.description: The number of days the patient stayed in the department during the current admission (enrollment). This field is calculated automatically.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Probiotic days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days the patient receives an oral probiotic containing at least one of Lactobacillus spp. or Bifidobacterium spp., regardless of the amount.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end PVC days
dataElements.description: The cumulative number of days when a peripheral venous catheter was in place for at least 12 hours/day.

dataElements.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Reason
dataElements.description: Reason for the end of surveillance. This can be either death or the discharge/transfer of the patient.

options.name: ASA I - A normal healthy patient
ASA II - A patient with mild systemic disease
ASA III - A patient with severe systemic disease
ASA IV - A patient with severe systemic disease that is a constant threat to life
ASA V - A moribund patient who is not expected to survive without the operation
Admitted from delivery room (initial admission for infants delivered in your hospital)
Transferred/readmitted to your hospital on the day of birth
Transferred/readmitted to your hospital the day after birth or later
...only skin and subcutaneous tissue of the incision
...tissue deeper than fascial/muscle layer, opened/manipulated during the procedure
North American eastern equine encephalitis virus [synonym]
South American eastern equine encephalitis virus [synonym]

optionSets.name: NeoIPC BSI Pathogen recovered from options
NeoIPC HAP Respiratory tract sample sources

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Identifier
trackedEntityAttributes.description: Use this identifier to uniquely identify a patient in the system. Ideally use an unique random string of characters. If have a requirement to identify a patient you have entered here, you can use this identifier as the NeoIPC key for pseudonymization. NEVER use an identifier that is used anywhere else and that you do not fully control (e.g. do NOT use the patient id from your hospital information system).

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Birth weight
trackedEntityAttributes.description: The infant’s weight immediately after birth in grams. Typically this value is based on a measurement but in case the birth weigth is unknown or has a highly pathological value that does not reflect the maturity of the infant (e.g. hydrops fetalis) you can enter the birth weight that is estimated by the treating physician.

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Delivery mode
trackedEntityAttributes.description: The mode of the infant's delivery. This can be either vaginal (including assisted vaginal delivery), elective caesarean section or emergency caesarean section.

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Gestational age
trackedEntityAttributes.description: The gestational age, expressed in completed weeks and days (e.g., 25 weeks and 4 days: 25+4) at the time of birth. Typically this refers to the gestational age as it was calculated or estimated by the mother's obstetrician but where this is not available (e.g. in unobserved pregnancies) the gestational age assesed by the treating physician (e.g. via the Ballard score) may be recorded.

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Multiple birth
trackedEntityAttributes.description: Check this field if the infant is part of a multiple birth.

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Sex
trackedEntityAttributes.description: Typically the phenotypic sex of the patient. If sex cannot be determined from the patient's phenotype or genotype, or if the genotype is neither XX nor XY, it is considered undetermined for purposes of surveillance.

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Infants in birth
trackedEntityAttributes.description: The number of infants delivered from the (multiple) pregnancy this infant belongs to. Please report the total number of infants including the one you are recording here.

trackedEntityAttributes.name: NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days
trackedEntityAttributes.description: The total number of completed gestation days at the time of birth. This field is automatically calculated from the gestational age field.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Admission Date must match enrollment admission date
programRules.description: Validates that the admission date in the enrollment and the admission date in the admission event are the same.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Admission Day of life 1
programRules.description: Causes an error on attempts to enter 1 as value for the admission day of life
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC Admission type - current event value} ) && #{NeoIPC Admission type - current event value}  == 3 && #{NeoIPC Admission on day of life value} < 2

programRules.name: NeoIPC Admission Day of life value 150+
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the day of life at the time of admission is 150 ore more
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Admission on day of life value} >= 150

programRules.name: NeoIPC Admission Set Length of Stay
programRules.description: Automatically sets the Length of Stay for the current event

programRules.name: NeoIPC Admission Set completed
NeoIPC Admission Type 1
programRules.description: Adjust the NeoIPC admission data entry form when the patient was delivered in the respective hospital or admitted on the day of birth
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC Admission type - current event value} ) || #{NeoIPC Admission type - current event value}  <= 2

programRules.name: NeoIPC Admission Type 2+
programRules.description: Adjust the NeoIPC admission data entry form when the patient was admitted on the day after birth or later
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC Admission type - current event value} ) && #{NeoIPC Admission type - current event value}  == 3

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI - unless no positive culture
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog when "No positive culture" is disabled.

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI - when no positive culture
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog when "No positive culture" is enabled

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Acidosis set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Acidosis count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Acidosis value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Acidosis value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Apnoea set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Apnoea count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Apnoea value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Apnoea value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Assign common laboratory findings count
programRules.description: Sets the variable for the common laboratory findings to 1 if any are set as present in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog.
programRules.condition:
(#{NeoIPC BSI White blood cell count count} > 0)
||
(#{NeoIPC BSI Platelet count count} > 0)
||
(#{NeoIPC BSI CRP count} > 0)
||
(#{NeoIPC BSI Procalcitonin count} > 0)
||
(#{NeoIPC BSI IT ratio count} > 0)
||
(#{NeoIPC BSI Interleukin count} > 0)

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Assign first finding counts
programRules.description: Counts the findings (except for the common laboratory findings that count as 1) that are set as present in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog and assigns the result to the variables relevant for the validation rule.

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Assign total common finding counts
programRules.description: Assigns the total value for the clinical finding counts.

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Bradycardia set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Bradycardia count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Bradycardia value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Bradycardia value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI CRP set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI CRP count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI CRP value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI CRP value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Clinical sepsis validation rule
programRules.description: Validates the findings in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog against the clinical sepsis definition in the protocol
programRules.condition:
(
  (d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI - no positive culture}) && (#{NeoIPC BSI - no positive culture} == true))
  &&
  (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 value}))
  &&
  (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 value}))
  &&
  (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 value}))
)
&&
(
  (#{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment} == false)
  ||
  (#{NeoIPC BSI Common sepsis total findings count} < 2)
)

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Day of life value less than 4
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the day of life at the time of a BSI event is 3 or less
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC BSI on day of life current value} < 4

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Debug rule
programRules.description: A rule that shows several key/value pairs for debugging purposes

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Feeding intolerance set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Feeding intolerance count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Feeding intolerance value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Feeding intolerance value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Hyperglycaemia  set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Hyperglycaemia  count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Hyperglycaemia  value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Hyperglycaemia  value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI IT ratio set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI I/T ratio count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI IT ratio value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI IT ratio value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Interleukin set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Interleukin count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Interleukin value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Interleukin value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Irritability set count
programRules.description: Sets NeoIPC BSI Irritability count to 1 if it is set to true.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC BSI Irritability value} ) && #{NeoIPC BSI Irritability value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI LCBSI with common commensal detected multiple times or with AB-treatment >= 5 days validation rule
programRules.description: Validates the findings in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog against the LCBSI definition in the protocol where a common commensal was detected multiple times or where systemic antibiotic treatment for 5 days or more was initiated.
programRules.condition:
(
  !#{NeoIPC BSI - no positive culture}
  &&
  (
    d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 value})
    ||
    d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 value})
    ||
    d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 value})
  )
  &&
  (
    !#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 is recognized pathogen}
    &&
    !#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 is recognized pathogen}
    &&
    !#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 is recognized pathogen}
  )
)
&&
(
  (
    (d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 recovered multiple times value}) && (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 recovered multiple times value} == true))
    ||
    (d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 recovered multiple times value}) && (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 recovered multiple times value} == true))
    ||
    (d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 recovered multiple times value}) && (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 recovered multiple times value} == true))
    ||
    (d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment}) && (#{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment} == true))
  )
  &&
  (
    #{NeoIPC BSI Common sepsis total findings count} < 2
  )
)

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI LCBSI with common commensal detected only once and no antibiotic treatment validation rule
programRules.description: Validates the findings in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog against the LCBSI definition in the protocol where a common commensal was detected only once and no antibiotic treatment was initiated.

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Length of Stay less than 2
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the length of stay at the time of a BSI event is 1 or less
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Admission type - value} == 3 && #{NeoIPC BSI Length of Stay current value} < 2

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 1 - name has value
programRules.description: Sends a message when a custom name is entered as Organism 1 in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 2 - name has value
programRules.description: Sends a message when a custom name is entered as Organism 2 in the NeoIPC Core BSI dialog

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Organism 3 - name has value

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Pathogen - when recognized pathogen
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 is recognized pathogen}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Pathogen - when recognized pathogen or antibiotic treatment
programRules.description: Hides the detected multiple times checkbox when a recognized pathogen was detected or an antibiotic treatment for the common commensal was initiated.
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment}

programRules.name: NeoIPC BSI Pathogen - when recognized pathogen or recovered multiple times
programRules.description: Hides the antibiotic treatment checkbox when a recognized pathogen was detected or the common commensal was detected multiple times
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 is recognized pathogen} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 recovered multiple times value} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 recovered multiple times value} || #{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 recovered multiple times value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Increased respiratory secretion value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC HAP Increased respiratory secretion value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC HAP Increased respiratory secretion value} ) && #{NeoIPC HAP Increased respiratory secretion value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Laboratory findings value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC HAP Laboratory findings value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC HAP Laboratory findings value} ) && #{NeoIPC HAP Laboratory findings value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Length of Stay less than 2
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the length of stay at the time of a HAP event is 1 or less
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Admission type - value} == 3 && #{NeoIPC HAP Length of Stay current value} < 2

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive - infer false
programRules.description: Sets the program rule variable "NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive" to false if none of the 3 detected pathogens was detected from the lower respiratory tract.
programRules.condition: !(#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 1 source value} > 1) && !(#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 2 source value} > 1) && !(#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 3 source value} > 1)

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive - infer true
programRules.description: Sets the program rule variable "NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive" to true if any of the 3 detected pathogens was detected from the lower respiratory tract.
programRules.condition: (#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 1 source value} > 1) || (#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 2 source value} > 1) || (#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 3 source value} > 1)

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Microbiological test result value - 1+

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Virus detected - infer false
programRules.description: Sets the program rule variable "NeoIPC HAP Virus detected" to false if none of the 3 detected pathogens is a virus.
programRules.condition: !#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 1 is virus} && !#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 2 is virus} && !#{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 3 is virus}

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Virus detected - infer true
programRules.description: Sets the program rule variable "NeoIPC HAP Virus detected" to true if any of the 3 detected pathogens is a virus.
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 1 is virus} || #{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 2 is virus} || #{NeoIPC HAP Pathogen 3 is virus}

programRules.name: NeoIPC HAP Virus detected or NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC HAP Virus detected" or "NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive" is true.
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC HAP Microbiological test result value} > 0 && (#{NeoIPC HAP Virus detected} || #{NeoIPC HAP Lower respiratory tract sample positive})

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC - assign finding counts
NeoIPC NEC - display feedback
NeoIPC NEC Abdominal discoloration value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Abdominal discoloration value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Abdominal discoloration value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Abdominal discoloration value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Abdominal distension value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Abdominal distension value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Abdominal distension value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Abdominal distension value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Bilious gastric aspirate value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Bilious gastric aspirate value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Bilious gastric aspirate value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Bilious gastric aspirate value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Blood in stool value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Blood in stool value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Blood in stool value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Blood in stool value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Day of life value less than 4
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the day of life at the time of a NEC event is 3 or less
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC NEC on day of life current value} < 4

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Extensive bowel necrosis value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Extensive bowel necrosis value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Extensive bowel necrosis value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Extensive bowel necrosis value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Fixed bowel loop value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Fixed bowel loop value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Fixed bowel loop value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Fixed bowel loop value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Imaging Clinical findings criterion fulfilled - assign false
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC NEC Imaging findings total count} < 1 || #{NeoIPC NEC Clinical findings total count} < 1

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Imaging Clinical findings criterion fulfilled - assign true
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC NEC Imaging findings total count} >= 1 && #{NeoIPC NEC Clinical findings total count} >= 1

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Increased gastric residuals value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Increased gastric residuals value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Increased gastric residuals value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Increased gastric residuals value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Length of Stay less than 2
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the length of stay at the time of a NEC event is 1 or less
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Admission type - value} == 3 && #{NeoIPC NEC Length of Stay current value} < 2

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis imaging value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis imaging value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis imaging value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis imaging value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis surgical value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis surgical value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis surgical value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Pneumatosis intestinalis surgical value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Pneumoperitoneum value - true

programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Pneumoperitoneum value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Pneumoperitoneum value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Portal venous gas value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Portal venous gas value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Portal venous gas value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Portal venous gas value}

programRules.description: Sends a message when a custom name is entered as Organism 1 in the secondary BSI section of the NeoIPC Core NEC dialog
programRules.condition: 0 < d2:length(#{NeoIPC NEC Secondary BSI pathogen 1 name value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Secondary bloodstream infection value - 1+
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Secondary bloodstream infection value" is greater than 0

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Secondary bloodstream infection value - no value or 0-
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Secondary bloodstream infection value" does not have a value or when the value is less than 1

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Set Day of life
programRules.description: Automatically sets the day of life for the current NEC event

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Set Length of Stay
programRules.description: Automatically sets the Length of Stay for the current event

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Surgical findings criterion fulfilled - assign false
NeoIPC NEC Surgical findings criterion fulfilled - assign true
NeoIPC NEC Validation rule
programRules.description: Validates the findings in the NeoIPC Core NEC dialog against the definition in the protocol
programRules.condition: !#{NeoIPC NEC Surgical findings criterion fulfilled} && !#{NeoIPC NEC Imaging Clinical findings criterion fulfilled}

programRules.name: NeoIPC NEC Vomiting value - true
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog and assigns variables when "NeoIPC NEC Vomiting value" is true
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( #{NeoIPC NEC Vomiting value} ) && #{NeoIPC NEC Vomiting value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Assign gestation days and weeks
programRules.description: Assigns the number of gestation days and weeks from the gestational age input format to program rule variables
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( A{NeoIPC Patient Gestational age value} ) && d2:validatePattern( A{NeoIPC Patient Gestational age value}, '^[2-4]\\d\\+[0-6]$' )

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Assign total gestation days
programRules.description: Calculates the total number of gestation days and weeks from the two program rule variables that contain the weeks and days.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Birth weight 1500+ grams and gestational age 32+ weeks
programRules.description: Raises an error if the entered birth weight is 1500 grams or more and the gestational age is 32 weeks or more.
programRules.condition: (( A{NeoIPC Patient Birth weight value} >= 1500 &&  #{NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days} >= 224 ) || ( A{NeoIPC Patient Birth weight value} >= 1500 && #{NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days} == 0 ) || ( #{NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days} >= 224 &&  A{NeoIPC Patient Birth weight value} == 0 )) && ( ! d2:inOrgUnitGroup( 'NEOIPC_ALL_PATIENTS_ELIGIBLE' ) )

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Birth weight and gestational age mandatory (NeoDeco)
programRules.description: Makes both birth weight and gestational age mandatory if the site is a NeoDeco trial site.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Birth weight mandatory if gestational age missing (regular)
programRules.description: Makes birth weight mandatory if gestational age is missing and the site is not a NeoDeco trial site.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Gestational age mandatory if birth weight missing (regular)
programRules.description: Makes gestational age mandatory if birth weight is missing and the site is not a NeoDeco trial site.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Multiple birth is not set
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog when the multiple birth checkbox is not checked.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Multiple birth is set
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog when the multiple birth checkbox is checked.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Show feedback for "Admission Completed"
NeoIPC Patient Validate gestational age format
programRules.description: Checks the format of the value entered into the gestational age input field
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue( A{NeoIPC Patient Gestational age value} ) && !d2:validatePattern( A{NeoIPC Patient Gestational age value}, '^[2-4]\\d\\+[0-6]$' )

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Warn birth weight 1-299 or 5000+
programRules.description: Issues a warning when the birth weight is less than 300 grams or more than 5000 grams.
programRules.condition: A{NeoIPC Patient Birth weight value} != 0 && (A{NeoIPC Patient Birth weight value} < 300 || A{NeoIPC Patient Birth weight value} >= 5000 )

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Warn birth weight empty (regular)
programRules.description: Issues a warning if the birth weight is empty.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Warn gestation days 0-160 or 310+
programRules.description: Displays a warning if the calculated number of gestation days is less than 161 or greater than 310
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days} != 0 && (#{NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days} < 161) || (#{NeoIPC Patient Total gestation days} >= 310)

programRules.name: NeoIPC Patient Warn siblings in birth 7+
programRules.description: Issues a warning when the number of siblings in one birth is 7 or more.
programRules.condition: A{NeoIPC Patient Siblings in birth value} >= 7

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - has microbiology results
programRules.description: Makes pathogen-related data elements mandatory if the user specifies that microbiology result are available
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from any site}) && #{NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from any site}

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - has secondary BSI
programRules.description: Makes data elements related to secondary BSI mandatory if the user specifies that a secondary BSI has occurred.
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC SSI Secondary bloodstream infection value}) && #{NeoIPC SSI Secondary bloodstream infection value} > 0

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - infection type deep
programRules.description: Hides data elements for superficial and organ/space infections if the infection type is deep

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - infection type organ/space
programRules.description: Hides data elements for superficial and superficial infections if the infection type is organ/space

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - infection type superficial
programRules.description: Hides data elements for deep and organ/space infections if the infection type is superficial

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - no infection type
programRules.description: Hides clinical signs and symptoms data elements until the user selects an infection type
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC SSI Infection type value}) || #{NeoIPC SSI Infection type value} < 1

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - no microbiology result available
programRules.description: Hides pathogen-related data elements unless the user specifies that microbiologic result are available
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from any site}) || !#{NeoIPC SSI Organisms identified from any site}

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - no secondary BSI
programRules.description: Hides data elements related to secondary BSI unless the user specifies that a secondary BSI has occurred.
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC SSI Secondary bloodstream infection value}) || #{NeoIPC SSI Secondary bloodstream infection value} < 1

programRules.name: NeoIPC SSI - set organisms identified from any site
NeoIPC SSI - superficial infection
programRules.description: Adjusts the dialog when only the skin is involved
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC SSI Infection involves skin value} && !#{NeoIPC SSI Infection involves deep soft tissues value} && !#{NeoIPC SSI Infection involves parts deeper than fascial muscular layers value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 - hide
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days - current event value} <= 0

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 - require
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days - current event value} >= 1

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 09 - hide
programRules.condition: !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 09 days - require
programRules.condition: d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 09 - current event value})

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance days - validate
programRules.condition: (#{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 01 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 02 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 03 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 04 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 05 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 06 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 07 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 08 days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Antibiotic substance 09 days - current event value}) < #{NeoIPC Surveillance end AB days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end CVC days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end CVC days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Human milk days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Human milk days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end INV days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end INV days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Kangaroo care days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Kangaroo care days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end NIV + INV days - validate
programRules.condition: (#{NeoIPC Surveillance end NIV days - current event value} + #{NeoIPC Surveillance end INV days - current event value})> #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end NIV days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end NIV days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end PVC days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end PVC days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - set
programRules.description: Automatically calculate the patient days.

programRules.name: NeoIPC Surveillance end Probiotic days - validate
programRules.condition: #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Probiotic days - current event value} > #{NeoIPC Surveillance end Patient days - current event value}

programRuleActions.content: For transferred or re-admitted patients an infection is only considered a nosocomial infection if the day of symptom onset is greater than or equal to day 3 of the patient’s  hospital stay, where the day of admission is regarded as day 1. Please see chapter 3.4 of the NeoIPC Surveillance Protocol for the rules.

programRuleActions.content: For transferred or re-admitted patients an infection is only considered a nosocomial infection if the day of symptom onset is greater than or equal to day 3 of the patient’s  hospital stay, where the day of admission is regarded as day 1. Please see chapter 3.4 of the NeoIPC Surveillance Protocol for the rules.

programRuleActions.content: The sum of all antibiotic substance days must be greater than or equal to antibiotic days

programRuleActions.data: #{NeoIPC BSI Common sepsis clinical findings count}
d2:daysBetween( V{enrollment_date}, V{event_date} ) + #{NeoIPC Admission on day of life value}

(
  (
    !d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI - no positive culture})
    ||
    (#{NeoIPC BSI - no positive culture} != true)
  )
  &&
  (
    d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 value})
    ||
    d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 value})
    ||
    d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 value})
  )
  &&
  (
    (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 is recognized pathogen} == false)
    &&
    (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 is recognized pathogen} == false)
    &&
    (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 is recognized pathogen} == false)
  )
)
&&
(
  (
    (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 recovered multiple times value}) || (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 1 recovered multiple times value} != true))
    &&
    (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 recovered multiple times value}) || (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 2 recovered multiple times value} != true))
    &&
    (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 recovered multiple times value}) || (#{NeoIPC BSI Pathogen 3 recovered multiple times value} != true))
    &&
    (!d2:hasValue(#{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment}) || (#{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment} != true))
  )
  &&
  (
    (#{NeoIPC BSI Common commensal once clinical findings count} < 2)
    ||
    (#{NeoIPC BSI Common commensal once laboratory findings count} < 1)
  )
)
!#{NeoIPC BSI - Antibiotic treatment}

programRuleActions.content: For transferred or re-admitted patients an infection is only considered a nosocomial infection if the day of symptom onset is greater than or equal to day 3 of the patient’s  hospital stay, where the day of admission is regarded as day 1. Please see chapter 3.4 of the NeoIPC Surveillance Protocol for the rules.

programStageSections.name: Enrolment's Admission Information
programStageSections.description: Information on the patient's admission this enrolment covers.

programStageSections.name: Surgical Procedure
programStageSections.description: Each time a surgical procedure is performed on an enrolled child, you must complete this form and start following up the patient for surgical site infections for 30 days (90 days if an implant was left in place during the procedure).

programStageSections.name: End of Surveillance
programStageSections.description: Information on cumulative risk factors that can be calculated (aggregated) after the surveillance for this patient has ended.

programStageSections.name: Imaging Findings
programStageSections.description: At least one of the following diagnostic imaging findings. Imaging diagnostics can be X-ray, CT, MRI or ultrasound.

programStageSections.name: Superficial Incisional SSI
programStageSections.description: The infection involves only skin and subcutaneous tissue of the incision and first symptoms occur within 30 days after the operation.

programStageSections.name: Clinical Signs and Symptoms
programStageSections.description: At least one of the following clinical signs or symptoms (with no other cause).

programStageSections.name: Deep Incisional SSI
programStageSections.description: The infection involves deep soft tissues of the incision (for example, fascial and muscle layers) and first symptoms occur within 30 days after the operation or 90 days after the operation when an implant was left in place.

programStageSections.name: Identified Organisms Recovered from the Respiratory Tract
Signs and Symptoms of Generalized Infection:
Organ/Space SSI
programStageSections.description: Infection involves any part of the body deeper than the fascial/muscle layers that is opened or manipulated during the operative procedure and first symptoms occur within 30 days after the operation or 90 days after the operation when an implant was left in place.

programStageSections.name: Surgical or Pathological Findings
programStageSections.description: Alternatively one of the following intraoperative findings from a surgeon or findings from a pathologist based on resections or an autopsy apply. Cases with surgical findings of intestinal perforation without evidence of primary necrosis or pneumatosis intestinalis (e.g., a spontaneous intestinal perforation) should not be reported as NEC.

programStageSections.name: Identified Organisms Recovered from the Surgical Site
programStageSections.description:
Organism(s) identified from the surgical site tissue by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, not Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).
For superficial incisional SSI this requires an aseptically obtained specimen from the superficial incision or subcutaneous tissue, for deep incisional SSI this requires a specimen from the deep soft tissues of the incision and for organ/space SSI this requires fluid or tissue from the organ/space.

programStageSections.name: Secondary Bloodstream Infection (BSI)/Sepsis
programStageSections.description: Secondary BSI that is thought to be seeded from the NEC or it's complications (e.g.; peritonitis after perforation). A BSI can be attributed to a NEC if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first NEC symptoms), 3 days prior, and 13 days after. Follow-up for secondary BSI is not mandatory.

programStageSections.name: Secondary bloodstream infection (BSI)
programStageSections.description: Secondary BSI that is thought to be seeded from the SSI. A BSI can be attributed to a SSI if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first SSI symptoms), 3 days prior, and 13 days after. Follow-up for secondary BSI is not mandatory.

programStages.name: Admission
programStages.description: The surveillance event where the patient admission is recorded.

programStages.name: Surgical Procedure
programStages.description: The surveillance event where a surgical procedure performed on a patient is recorded.

programStages.name: Primary Sepsis/BSI
programStages.description: The surveillance event where a primiary blood stream infection (including clinical sepsis) is recorded.

programStages.name: Necrotizing enterocolitis
programStages.description: The surveillance event where a necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC) is recorded.

programStages.name: Surgical Site Infection
programStages.description: The surveillance event where a surgical site infection is recorded.

programStages.name: Pneumonia
programStages.description: The surveillance event where a pneumonia is recorded.

programStages.name: Surveillance-End
programStages.description: The surveillance event where the end of surveillance and the aggregated risk factors are recorded.

programIndicators.name: NeoIPC Admission Completed
programIndicators.description: 1 if the admission is completed, otherwise 0.

programIndicators.name: NeoIPC Day of life
programIndicators.description: The day of life when an event occurs

programIndicators.name: NeoIPC Enrollment Completed
programIndicators.description: 1 if the enrollment is completed, otherwise 0.
